tooling_result.reason: Focused windows, multiseed field tracing, call extraction, and literal extraction were sufficient to isolate the selector body and its URL helper evidence.

field_trace_evidence.meaning: The selector 0x12E body roots its route or URL source at request/event +0xD0.
The helper path is gated by a controller child +0x68 flag.
When the gate passes, the path reads a controller child +0x60 service or route consumer before building the route argument.
Writes controller child state 0x2 after the helper route call path.
Writes controller child state 0x3 after the immediate external/open fallback path.

gate_sequence.role: Loads a global service/provider descriptor before an indirect readiness check.
Resolves or queries the service/provider descriptor.
Virtual readiness/availability check; a false result branches to the inline fallback at 0x14029E84C.
A zero child +0x68 flag also branches to the inline fallback.

build_and_call.role: Loads the child route service source.
Builds or normalizes a temporary string from request/event +0xD0.
Invokes the route/open helper with the child +0x60-derived service and the +0xD0 route string.

predicate.meaning: The predicate helper recognizes http and https URL prefixes.

arguments_observed: R8 = request/event +0xD0 route or URL pointer
shell_execute_call.bounded_inference: This is the client's external browser/open fallback for URL-like route material.

negative_evidence: This selector body does not read request/event +0x80/+0x88; it roots route material at request/event +0xD0 instead.
This selector body does not write controller child +0x38; the observed child writes are state writes to [RDI].

confirmed: The selector body uses request/event +0xD0 as the route or URL source.
A service/provider readiness check and child +0x68 gate decide whether the path calls the helper route opener through child +0x60-derived state.
The fallback path checks for http and https URL prefixes and can call SHELL32.dll!ShellExecuteA with verb "open".
The route-helper path writes controller child state 0x2, while the inline external/open fallback writes state 0x3.
reversing_result.bounded_inference: Selector 0x12E is the client's web-login route/open continuation. It consumes a request/event +0xD0 route string and either hands it to an internal route/open helper or falls back to opening an external URL.
reversing_result.opennxt_relevance: For OpenNXT 947, the server/web handoff should treat selector 0x12E as the client-side open-route response. The request/event object must carry the route or URL at +0xD0, distinct from selector 0xC8's authCode span and selector 0x1A6's social code span.

next_targets: Trace shared tail 0x14029EA3C/0x14029EA2E to identify the side effects after states 0x2 and 0x3 are written.
Trace child +0x60 producer/callers to name the route/open service object more precisely.
Trace request/event +0xD0 producers to connect the native route string back to the web/login response construction.
